description: Baseline fresh-eye audit setup: added regression coverage for merge verify default and canonical feature naming, plus a new failing audit for the misleading 'set it as active' feature-tool claim. The initial harness exposes the intended metric but still exits nonzero when audits fail, so the next iteration should normalize the benchmark script to report audit_failures without treating the baseline as a crash.

Fixed the misleading feature-create contract by removing the 'set it as active' claim from FeatureTools metadata, and normalized autoresearch.sh so failing audits are reported via audit_failures instead of crashing the benchmark harness. The focused audit suite now passes cleanly and full lint/test checks stay green.

name: Fresh-eyes bug hunt across opencode-warcraft (doctor resilience audit)

description: Expanded the audit workload with a new DoctorTools resilience check for worktree-inspection failures. The issue is real, but placing the failing regression inside the main *.test.ts suite caused the global correctness checks to fail before the implementation existed. Discarding this shape and reintroducing the audit in a benchmark-only harness will keep the repository green while still driving the bug fix.

name: Fresh-eyes bug hunt across opencode-warcraft (doctor resilience eval)

description: Re-scoped the fresh-eye workload to a benchmark-only DoctorTools resilience eval outside the package test suites. The new audit exposes a real bug: doctorTool crashes instead of degrading gracefully when worktree inspection fails for a feature, while full lint and package tests remain green.

Made DoctorTools fail-soft around per-feature worktree inspection by catching listAll errors and surfacing them as a stale_worktrees warning instead of crashing the diagnostic tool. Added permanent regression coverage in doctor-tool.test.ts and kept the benchmark-only eval to ensure the degraded path stays green while full lint/test checks continue to pass.

Extended DoctorTools fail-soft behavior to feature enumeration as well as worktree inspection. The diagnostic tool now converts feature inventory crashes into a top-level feature_inventory warning instead of aborting, with permanent package coverage and benchmark eval coverage for both degraded paths.

name: Fresh-eyes bug hunt across opencode-warcraft (context status resilience eval)

description: Expanded the fresh-eye audit scope to ContextTools resilience and fixed warcraft_status so worktree inventory failures degrade to a worktreeHygiene warning instead of crashing status rendering. Added permanent package coverage plus a benchmark-only eval for the degraded path, with lint and full tests still passing.

name: Fresh-eyes bug hunt across opencode-warcraft (context workspace resilience eval)

description: Expanded ContextTools resilience to per-task workspace rendering. warcraft_status now swallows worktree lookup failures for individual tasks and reports workspace: null instead of crashing the whole status payload. Added permanent package coverage and a benchmark-only eval for the degraded per-task worktree path, while lint and full test suites remain green.

name: Fresh-eyes bug hunt across opencode-warcraft (worktree delegation contract audit)

description: Expanded the fresh-eye audit workload with a new worktree delegation contract check for the runtime system prompt. The new benchmark-only audit exposed a real orchestration bug: index.ts still told agents that warcraft_worktree_create auto-spawns a worker even though the tool actually returns a task() payload that must be issued explicitly. Full lint/test checks stayed green, so the next iteration can fix the prompt contract directly.

Fixed the runtime worktree delegation contract so the system prompt now accurately says warcraft_worktree_create returns a task() payload that must be issued explicitly, including the blocked-resume path and post-task guidance. Added benchmark-only and package-level regression coverage to keep the prompt contract aligned with actual tool behavior, with lint and full tests still green.

name: Fresh-eyes bug hunt across opencode-warcraft (worktree tool metadata audit)

description: Expanded the audit suite with a worktree tool metadata check, but the first benchmark shape was wrong: the new eval imported warcraft-core via a bare specifier from eval/, which fails under the current Bun test resolution for benchmark-only files. Discarding this harness-only regression and fixing the audit to avoid the bad import before measuring the actual metadata contract.

Aligned warcraft_worktree_create tool metadata with actual delegation behavior: the tool description now says it creates/reuses a task workspace and returns the task() payload needed to launch Mekkatorque, instead of falsely claiming automatic worker spawning. Added benchmark-only and package-level regression coverage so both the runtime system prompt and the tool metadata stay honest, with lint and full tests passing.

Expanded the audit workload to agent prompts and found two more stale delegation contracts: both Khadgar and Saurfang still describe warcraft_worktree_create as if it directly launches Mekkatorque, instead of returning a task() payload that the orchestrator must issue explicitly. This is a real workflow-facing prompt bug that can mislead orchestration even though the system prompt and tool metadata were already fixed, and full lint/test checks remain green.

Fixed the Khadgar and Saurfang prompts to describe warcraft_worktree_create as returning a task() payload, but the new benchmark audit was too brittle: it required the exact backticked phrase 'Issue the returned `task()` call' while the prompts already used the equivalent unbackticked wording. Discarding this harness-shaped failure and relaxing the regression checks to measure the real delegation-contract bug instead of formatting.

Fixed the stale Khadgar and Saurfang worktree delegation contracts so both agent prompts now describe warcraft_worktree_create as returning the task() payload for Mekkatorque and explicitly tell the orchestrator to issue that returned task() call. Added benchmark-only and package-level regression coverage, while relaxing the audit wording to avoid overfitting on backtick formatting, and lint/full tests remain green.

Expanded the audit workload to the shared blocker-handling fragment and found a fresh orchestration bug there: BLOCKER_PROTOCOL still stops at warcraft_worktree_create({ continueFrom: "blocked" }) and never tells Khadgar/Saurfang to issue the returned task() call that actually relaunches the worker. This is a real blocked-resume contract gap in the shared prompt fragment, while lint and full tests remain green.

Fixed the shared blocked-resume delegation contract in agents/fragments/blocker-handling.ts so orchestrators are explicitly told to issue the returned task() call after warcraft_worktree_create(... continueFrom: "blocked" ...) relaunches a worker. Added benchmark-only and package-level regression coverage for the shared blocker protocol, with lint and full tests still passing.

Expanded the audit workload to the top-level system workflow line and exposed another stale delegation contract: the summary workflow in index.ts still says warcraft_worktree_create(task) → work in worktree → warcraft_worktree_commit(...) instead of showing the returned task() call. Checks failed only because the new benchmark-only audit file needs Biome formatting, so the next iteration should keep the audit but fix both the prompt line and the test formatting.

Fixed the stale top-level workflow summary in index.ts so the quick-start flow now shows warcraft_worktree_create(task) → issue returned task() call → warcraft_worktree_commit(...), matching the real delegation contract instead of implying direct in-worktree execution. Added benchmark-only and package-level regression coverage, and formatted the new eval correctly so lint/full tests stay green.

Expanded the audit workload to Saurfang's descriptive metadata and found two more stale contracts: both the exported saurfangAgent description and the registry/plugin-config descriptions still say Saurfang 'spawns workers' directly, even though orchestration now works by issuing the task() payload returned from warcraft_worktree_create. Checks failed only because the new benchmark-only audit file needs Biome formatting, so the next iteration should keep this audit and fix the stale metadata strings plus formatting.

Fixed the stale Saurfang metadata strings, but the new package-level regression was shaped incorrectly: it assumed plugin config always emits a saurfang agent entry, which is false in unified mode where Khadgar is the primary orchestrator. The underlying metadata fix is still valid; discard this test shape and replace it with a split-mode-aware or source-level assertion before remeasuring.

Fixed the stale Saurfang metadata descriptions across exported agent metadata, the agent registry, and plugin-config registration so they no longer claim Saurfang directly spawns workers. The wording now reflects the real orchestration contract: Saurfang launches returned task() payloads. Added benchmark-only and package-level regression coverage, with lint and full tests green.

Expanded the audit workload to Mekkatorque metadata and found another stale contract: the worker is still described in both the agent registry and plugin-config registration as always executing in isolated worktrees, even though direct mode is a supported runtime path. This is a real user-facing metadata bug that can mislead orchestration and debugging, while lint and full tests remain green.

Fixed the stale Mekkatorque metadata so the worker is no longer described as always executing in isolated worktrees. The agent registry and plugin-config registration now describe execution in the assigned workspace (worktree or direct mode), which matches the real runtime behavior. Added benchmark-only and package-level regression coverage, with lint and full tests passing.

Expanded the audit workload into the generated skills registry and found three more stale workspace-contract bugs in user-facing skill content: the using-git-worktrees skill still says warcraft_worktree_create always creates an isolated worktree, the warcraft skill still says Mekkatorque executes tasks in worktrees, and the blocked-resume skill guidance still omits the returned task() call after warcraft_worktree_create(... continueFrom: "blocked" ...). These are real workflow-facing documentation bugs, while lint and full tests remain green.

Fixed most of the stale workspace-contract skill content: generated skills no longer claim warcraft_worktree_create always creates an isolated worktree, and the warcraft skill now describes Mekkatorque as running in the assigned workspace instead of only in worktrees. One real gap remains: the generated blocked-resume guidance still does not match the audit's returned-task-call expectation, so the next iteration should inspect the regenerated wording and align that final resume contract without overfitting.

Updated the skill sources and regenerated the registry, but the remaining benchmark failure is harness-shaped rather than product-shaped: the generated registry clearly contains the blocked-resume guidance, and the audit is tripping over raw source-string matching details in the generated TypeScript file. Discarding this measurement and simplifying the audit to assert only the meaningful regenerated phrase instead of brittle source encoding details.

Fixed the stale generated skill guidance by updating the source skills and regenerating the built-in registry. using-git-worktrees now describes Warcraft-managed task workspaces and the returned task() call, while the warcraft skill now describes Mekkatorque as executing in the assigned workspace and tells blocked-resume flows to issue the newly returned task() call. Added package-level and benchmark-only regression coverage, while simplifying one brittle source-encoding check to avoid overfitting.

Expanded the audit workload to two more generated skills and found direct-mode compatibility drift in both: dispatching-parallel-agents still says parallel Warcraft dispatch runs in isolated worktrees, and subagent-driven-development still says warcraft_worktree_create always starts each task in an isolated workspace. These are real workflow-facing skill bugs because direct mode is a supported runtime path, while lint and full tests remain green.

Fixed the stale direct-mode compatibility guidance in generated skills by updating the source skill docs and regenerating the registry. dispatching-parallel-agents now explains that each warcraft_worktree_create call prepares an assigned workspace and returns a task() payload, while subagent-driven-development now tells users to issue the returned task() call in the assigned workspace instead of assuming isolated worktrees. Added package-level and benchmark-only regression coverage, with lint and full tests passing.

Expanded the audit workload to the remaining skill workspace wording and found two more stale contracts in generated user-facing skills: the warcraft skill still says '[Mekkatorque implements in worktree]' for single-task execution, and writing-plans still tells users to open the follow-on execution session specifically 'in worktree' even though direct mode is supported. These are real workflow-facing documentation bugs, while lint and full tests remain green.

Fixed the remaining stale workspace wording in generated skills by updating the source docs and regenerating the registry. The warcraft skill now says Mekkatorque implements in the assigned workspace, and writing-plans now tells users to open the follow-on execution session in the appropriate Warcraft-managed workspace instead of a worktree specifically. Added package-level and benchmark-only regression coverage, with lint and full tests passing.

Expanded the audit workload to the last remaining skill workspace wording and found two more stale phrases in generated skills: the warcraft skill still says warcraft_worktree_create 'creates the worktree' in one section and its tool table still says 'Create worktree + delegation instructions', while subagent-driven-development still references setting up an 'isolated workspace before starting'. Checks failed only because the new benchmark-only audit file needed Biome formatting, so the next iteration should keep this audit and fix the remaining skill wording.

Fixed the last remaining stale workspace wording in generated skills by updating the source docs and regenerating the registry. The warcraft skill now says warcraft_worktree_create prepares the task workspace and labels it consistently in the tool table, while subagent-driven-development now tells users to set up the Warcraft-managed workspace instead of an isolated workspace unconditionally. Added package-level and benchmark-only regression coverage, with lint and full tests passing.

Expanded the audit workload to warcraft_status nextAction guidance and found another stale orchestration contract: when exactly one task is runnable, ContextTools still tells users only to 'Start next task with warcraft_worktree_create' and omits the required follow-up to issue the returned task() call. This is a real user-facing workflow bug in status guidance, while lint and full tests remain green.

Fixed the stale warcraft_status nextAction guidance so a single runnable task now instructs users to call warcraft_worktree_create and then issue the returned task() call, matching the real delegation contract instead of stopping halfway. Added benchmark-only and package-level regression coverage, with lint and full tests passing.

Expanded the audit workload to parallel nextAction guidance and found another stale orchestration contract in ContextTools: when multiple tasks are runnable, warcraft_status still says they are 'ready to start in parallel' but does not direct users toward warcraft_batch_execute preview/execute or the required follow-up to issue all returned task() calls in the same assistant message. This is a real user-facing workflow bug, while lint and full tests remain green.

Fixed the stale parallel nextAction guidance in warcraft_status so multiple runnable tasks now direct users to warcraft_batch_execute preview/execute and to issue all returned task() calls in the same assistant message, instead of vaguely saying tasks are ready to start in parallel. Added benchmark-only and package-level regression coverage, with lint and full tests passing.

Expanded the audit workload to TaskTools user-facing messaging and found three stale delegation contracts: createTaskTool success message says 'to use its worktree' instead of the generic assigned workspace and omits the returned task() call, and updateTaskTool reopen-rejection tells users to 'use warcraft_worktree_create' without mentioning the returned task() call. Checks failed due to Biome formatting on the new audit file.

Fixed three stale delegation contracts in TaskTools: createTaskTool success message no longer says 'to use its worktree' and now directs users to issue the returned task() call in the assigned workspace; updateTaskTool reopen-rejection now mentions the returned task() call after warcraft_worktree_create. Added benchmark-only and package-level regression coverage, with lint and full tests passing.

Fixed two stale delegation contracts in WorktreeTools: the blocked-task message now mentions issuing the returned task() call after warcraft_worktree_create resume, and the missing-baseCommit error now says 'workspace' instead of 'worktree' and mentions the returned task() call after recreation. Added benchmark-only and package-level regression coverage, with lint and full tests passing.
